method: Read-only in-app browser navigation and DOM layout measurement at a temporary 640x320 CSS-pixel landscape viewport. No consent decision, form submission, authentication, administrative action, or production data write occurred. The viewport was reset and the temporary tab was closed after testing.

directory.note: The analytics-consent dialog was visible. It was not interacted with; its informational inline link is not counted as a primary control.

conclusion: No horizontal page overflow was observed in the measured populated directory or representative detail route at the landscape viewport. This is narrow visual evidence only.
limitations: This does not prove native 200% browser zoom, real or emulated touch, keyboard dispatch, screen-reader behavior, safe-area insets, reduced motion, virtual-keyboard behavior, constrained-network behavior, or complete form journeys.
This does not establish WCAG conformance or close the customer_journeys release gate by itself.
